task_description: Search for a one-way first-class flight for one traveler from Shenzhen to Shanghai on the selected departure date.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the Flights category tab, identified by a blue-and-yellow airplane icon above the text label 'Flights'. This is a top-area navigation control, not a canvas object. In the full screen, the Expedia homepage is open with a horizontal row of travel categories near the top: Stays, Flights, Cars, Packages, Things to do, and Cruises. Below it is the search form with destination, dates, travelers, and a blue Search button. The Flights tab appears to be the intended target in the category selector.
caption.think: The user likely wants to switch the search mode from the currently shown category to flight booking by selecting the Flights tab in Expedia's top travel-category bar.
caption.action: Click the Flights tab with the airplane icon.
caption.expectation: The Flights category becomes selected, and the search panel should update to flight-specific fields or enter flight search mode.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the right side of the outlined input box labeled 'Travelers, Cabin class', specifically over the text '1 traveler, Economy'. The box has a person silhouette icon on the left and a blue border, indicating it is an interactive form control. In the full-screen Expedia Flights search panel, this control sits to the right of the date field and just left of the blue 'Search' button, within the top booking form under the selected 'Flights' tab.
caption.think: The user likely wants to open the traveler and cabin class selector to adjust the number of passengers or change the travel class before searching for flights.
caption.action: Click the 'Travelers, Cabin class' field showing '1 traveler, Economy'.
caption.expectation: The travelers and cabin class picker should open, likely as a dropdown or popover, allowing selection of passenger counts and cabin class.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered inside the outlined dropdown field labeled 'Cabin class', over the current value 'Economy'. The field has a blue focus border and a small downward chevron on the right, indicating it is a clickable UI control that opens a list. Just below it is a blue 'Done' button. In the full-screen Expedia Flights search page, this field appears inside the open 'Travelers and Cabin class' popover on the right side of the flight search form, where traveler counts and cabin class can be adjusted.
caption.think: The user likely wants to change the flight cabin class from Economy by opening the cabin class dropdown within the travelers popover.
caption.action: Click the 'Cabin class' dropdown field showing 'Economy'.
caption.expectation: The cabin class dropdown should open, showing selectable fare classes such as Economy and other available cabin options.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the menu item labeled 'First class' inside an open dropdown list. The item is highlighted with a dark gray selection background and white text, indicating it is a clickable option in a list rather than a blank area. In the full-screen Expedia Flights page, this dropdown is open within the 'Travelers and Cabin class' popover on the right side of the flight search form, beneath the 'Cabin class' field that currently shows 'Economy'.
caption.think: The user likely wants to change the flight cabin class from Economy to First class by selecting that option from the open cabin class dropdown.
caption.action: Click the 'First class' option in the cabin class dropdown list.
caption.expectation: The dropdown should close, 'Cabin class' should update to 'First class', and the travelers popover should remain open with the new cabin class selected.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the dark blue rounded button labeled 'Done' in white text at the bottom-right of the open popover. This is a UI control button. In the full-screen Expedia Flights page, the 'Travelers and Cabin class' popover is open over the search form, and the travelers field in the form already shows '1 traveler, First class,' indicating the cabin class change has been made and now needs to be confirmed.
caption.think: The user likely wants to confirm the updated traveler and cabin class settings and close the popover so they can continue with the flight search.
caption.action: Click the blue 'Done' button in the 'Travelers and Cabin class' popover.
caption.expectation: The popover should close immediately, leaving the travelers field updated to '1 traveler, First class' on the main search form.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the 'One-way' tab in the trip-type selector. It is a top-area UI control tab between 'Roundtrip' and 'Multi-city', with a blue underline currently under 'Roundtrip'. In the full-screen Expedia Flights search page, the flights search form is visible with 'Leaving from' filled as 'Guangzhou (CAN-Baiyun Intl.)', an empty 'Going to' field, dates, and '1 traveler, First class'. The click target is the trip-type tab row above the search fields.
caption.think: The user likely wants to switch the flight search from roundtrip to one-way before entering the destination and continuing the search setup.
caption.expectation: The trip type should change to one-way, the 'One-way' tab should become selected with the active underline, and the search form should update accordingly, likely removing the return-date requirement.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered inside the active 'Leaving from' input field, over the existing text 'Guangzhou (CAN-Baiyun Intl.)'. The field has a blue outline, indicating it is a selectable form control in the top search area, not a blank region. In the full-screen Expedia Flights page, the 'Flights' section is active and 'One-way' is already selected. The search form shows 'Leaving from' filled with Guangzhou, an empty 'Going to' field, a date field, a travelers/cabin-class field, and a blue 'Search' button.
caption.think: The user likely wants to focus the departure-airport field again, probably to edit or replace the current origin before continuing the one-way flight search.
caption.action: Click inside the 'Leaving from' field containing 'Guangzhou (CAN-Baiyun Intl.)'.
caption.expectation: The departure field should gain focus more explicitly, with the cursor placed in the field or the airport picker opening so the existing origin can be edited or changed.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The visible text entry area is the active 'Leaving from' flight-origin field, outlined in blue, with a location-pin icon on the left and a small circular clear button with an 'X' on the right. The text being entered in the field is 'shenzhe', indicating the user is typing a new departure city name and replacing the previous origin rather than appending to the earlier 'Guangzhou (CAN-Baiyun Intl.)' value. A large light-colored autocomplete dropdown is open directly beneath this field, though its suggestions are still blank or loading. In the full-screen Expedia Flights page, the 'Flights' section is selected, 'One-way' is active, the adjacent 'Going to' field is empty, the date field shows 'Thu, Apr 30', the travelers field shows '1 traveler, Economy', and a blue 'Search' button is to the right.
caption.think: The user is changing the departure airport/city for a one-way flight search and is typing Shenzhen into the origin field so matching airport suggestions can appear.
caption.action: Type 'SHENZHEN' into the active 'Leaving from' field.
caption.expectation: The origin field should update to the typed city name, and autocomplete suggestions for Shenzhen airports or locations should appear in the dropdown below.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the top autocomplete suggestion row in the dropdown beneath the 'Leaving from' field. The highlighted row has an airplane icon on the left and the full label 'Shenzhen (SZX - All Airports)' with 'China' underneath, indicating a selectable airport/city suggestion. In the full-screen Expedia Flights page, the Flights tab and One-way option are selected, the 'Leaving from' field contains 'shenzhen', the suggestions dropdown is open under that field, and the rest of the search form shows an empty 'Going to' field, date, travelers/cabin class, and a blue Search button.
caption.think: The user is choosing the suggested departure location that matches the typed origin text, specifically the all-airports Shenzhen option, so the origin field is filled with a valid selectable airport group.
caption.action: Click the highlighted autocomplete suggestion 'Shenzhen (SZX - All Airports)'.
caption.expectation: That suggestion should be selected, the dropdown should close, and the 'Leaving from' field should update to the chosen Shenzhen all-airports departure option.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop centers on the active destination text field labeled 'Going to'. The field has a blue focus outline, a location-pin icon on the left, a small circular clear button with an 'X' on the right, and the typed text visible as 'shangha', indicating destination text is being entered into the field rather than clicking a suggestion. Directly beneath it, an autocomplete dropdown is open showing matches including 'Shanghai (SHA - All Airports)' at the top, followed by 'Shanghai (PVG - Pudong Intl.)' and 'Shanghai (SHA - Hongqiao Intl.)'. In the full-screen Expedia Flights page, the Flights tab and One-way option are selected, the 'Leaving from' field is already set to 'Shenzhen, China (SZX-All Air...)', the date is 'Thu, Apr 30', travelers are '1 traveler, Economy', and a blue 'Search' button is on the right.
caption.think: The user is filling the destination field for a one-way flight search from Shenzhen and is typing Shanghai so the correct Shanghai airport or all-airports destination can be selected from the suggestions.
caption.action: Type 'SHANGHAI' into the active 'Going to' field, replacing the current partial text with the full city name.
caption.expectation: The destination field should show the completed Shanghai text, and the autocomplete dropdown should refresh or remain open with Shanghai destination options ready to select.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the first autocomplete suggestion directly below the active 'Going to' field. The highlighted row contains an airplane-style airport icon and the full label 'Shanghai (SHA - All Airports)' with 'China' beneath it, indicating a selectable destination suggestion. In the full-screen Expedia Flights page, the Flights tab and One-way option are selected, the 'Leaving from' field is already set to Shenzhen, the 'Going to' field contains 'shanghai', the suggestion dropdown is open with multiple Shanghai options, and the blue Search button is to the right.
caption.think: The user has typed the destination city and now wants to select the matching all-airports Shanghai destination from the autocomplete list so the flight search form uses a valid destination.
caption.action: Click the autocomplete suggestion 'Shanghai (SHA - All Airports)'.
caption.expectation: The Shanghai all-airports destination should be selected, the dropdown should close, and the 'Going to' field should update to the chosen Shanghai destination.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the date number '15' inside the calendar popup. The number is inside a thin dark circular outline, indicating an individual selectable day cell rather than a toolbar control or handle. In the full-screen Expedia Flights page, the Flights tab and One-way option are selected, the route is set from Shenzhen to Shanghai, and the date picker overlay is open showing April 2026 on the left and May 2026 on the right. The currently selected date appears as a solid blue circle on '30', and the click target is the April 15 day cell in the calendar.
caption.think: The user is choosing a different departure date for the one-way flight search, changing it from April 30 to April 15 by selecting that day in the open calendar.
caption.action: Click the day cell '15' in the calendar popup.
caption.expectation: The April 15 date should become selected in the date picker, replacing the previous highlighted date, while the calendar remains open or updates to reflect the new chosen departure date.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the dark blue rounded button labeled 'Done' at the bottom-right of the date-picker panel. This is a UI control, not a blank area or canvas object. In the full-screen Expedia Flights page, the One-way flight form is filled from Shenzhen to Shanghai, the calendar overlay is open, and the newly selected departure date is shown as 'Wed, Apr 15' with the day 15 highlighted in blue in the April calendar.
caption.think: The user has finished choosing the departure date and wants to confirm the date selection by closing the calendar picker so they can continue with the flight search form.
caption.action: Click the 'Done' button in the date picker.
caption.expectation: The selected date should be confirmed, and the calendar popup should close, returning focus to the main flight search form with the departure date set to Wed, Apr 15.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on a dark blue rounded rectangular button labeled 'Search' in white text. This is a clickable UI control, not a blank area, canvas object, or handle. In the full-screen Expedia Flights page, the Flights tab and One-way option are selected, the route fields are filled with Shenzhen, China as the origin and Shanghai, China (SHA-All Airports) as the destination, the date field shows 'Wed, Apr 15', and the travelers field shows '1 traveler, Economy'. The blue Search button sits at the right end of the flight search form.
caption.think: The user has completed the one-way flight search form and now wants to submit it to view available flights matching the selected route, date, and traveler settings.
caption.expectation: The flight search form should submit, and the page should begin loading or navigate to the flight results list for the specified Shenzhen to Shanghai trip on Wed, Apr 15.
